content:
💡 ghgrab | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: To efficiently download specific assets from GitHub repositories directly from the command line, avoiding unnecessary cloning and streamlining workflows.
✨ Key Features: • Search and download files/folders from GitHub via CLI • TUI (Terminal User Interface) with fuzzy search • File preview within the TUI • Support for GitHub LFS • Batch download mode
📖 Summary: ghgrab is a Rust-based command-line tool that provides a user-friendly terminal interface for searching and downloading specific files or entire folders from GitHub repositories without needing to perform a full `git clone`. It offers features like fuzzy search, file preview, batch downloads, and an agent mode for scripting.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
